steps: Go to https://developer.x.com and sign in with your X (Twitter) account
Create a Project and App in the developer portal if you don't already have one
Enable 'OAuth 2.0' in your App's 'User authentication settings'
Submit a request for Ads API access at https://ads.x.com/help — select your developer App and describe your use case
Wait for approval (typically a few business days)
Once approved, go to your App's 'Keys and tokens' tab to retrieve your Client ID and Client Secret
You will also need a valid X Ads account ID (visible in ads.x.com as account_id)

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID
credential_fields.description: OAuth 2.0 Client ID from the App's Keys and tokens tab

credential_fields.name: client_secret
credential_fields.label: Client Secret
credential_fields.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Secret — shown only once at creation

notes: Ads API access requires a separate approval — apply at https://ads.x.com/help
You must have an active X Ads account to use the Ads API
The Client Secret is shown only once — save it immediately
The Ads API base URL is https://ads-api.twitter.com (different from the main Twitter API)
Ads API uses OAuth 2.0 Bearer Token and user-context authentication
